calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jul-07-2015 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES
Off Calendar Jul-07-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mary Mayo for Rosalie Acosta. Judith Baer in Propria Persona.

The Court stated that there are a few issues on the first and final accounting and Ms. Baer's petition for fees and that there is an issue with the request to distribute directly to the beneficiaries of the trust instead of the trustee. Ms. Mayo responded that Rosalie Acosta is the trustee and that everything can be distributed to her, and explained that the previous trustee place all of the money in the Umpqua bank, that the current trustee has had a hard time getting her name on the account and moving the money. Ms. Baer informed the court that Ms. Acosta was on the account from the beginning, that the money was to be moved to other accounts but had not been done prior to Ms. Baer being dismissed, that Mr. Vickroy was not entitled to administrator fees per the agreement that he would resign retroactively as if he had never served. The Court asked about attorney's fees both allocation of statutory fees and extraordinary fees. Ms. Mayo stated that there are unfounded allegations and that separate time sheets have been kept in the estate and trust matters. Ms. Baer reported that she spent 48.4 hours of statutory service just to get Mr. Vickroy and Ms. Acosta appointed and to get the cash into a bank account and that Ms. Mayo's fees have to do with the preparation of the inventory & appraisal and this petition. Ms. Mayo stated that the creditor claim fell into the probate case, that the trust assets were converted and that the court is in the best position to allocate the statutory fees. Ms. Baer reported that in regards to the extraordinary fees 99% of the work was against the creditor claim of Mr. Vickroy, that a civil action was filed, that Ms. Mayo filed a separate action in probate regarding the creditor claim and that she tried to facilitate a settlement on behalf of the beneficiaries regarding the real property which ended up being trust property. Ms. Mayo stated that she agrees with Ms. Baer in that any extraordinary fees are allocated to the probate and not the trust, that her client feels that they are excessive, that the creditor claim did not include the property it dealt with the care for the decedent and other monies that were allegedly converted. The court stated that it concludes that the fees were related to the trust and not the probate. Ms. Baer stated that she performed 90% of the statutory services, that there is a declaration by Mr. Vickroys attorney on her behalf, that 100% of the fees are against the probate matter, that any issue involving real property also involved the creditor claim which was against the estate and that the global settlement included the trust. The two accounting petitions were taken under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

Petition 1. To Determine Ownership Of Estate Property And Directing Return Of Estate Property; 2. Fraud And Concealment;...
Off Calendar Jul-29-2014 Continued To Sep-16-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mary Mayo for (petitioner) Rosalie Acosta. Phil Foster for (respondent) Leonard Vickroy.

Ms. Mayo stated that counsel agreed to go before the court's settlement officer. The court will contact Ms. Ly (court settlement officer) and send out notice of a settlement conference for a date in August. Counsel indicated that nothing is immediately needed and that they both agreed to no spending of estate money. The petition to determine ownership of estate property and directing return of estate property, etc. was continued to September 16, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy A. Allen. (Not Reported)
